changes: ZeroClaw shipped fresh same-day releases again: stable v0.5.7 plus v0.5.7-beta.554, which keeps the Rust lightweight lane among the fastest-moving branches in the field.
AstrBot moved to v4.21.0, reinforcing that the broader IM-native agent platform space is still shipping quickly, not cooling off.

OpenClaw still did not cut a newer stable release than v2026.3.13-1, but its official showcase remains the most important strategic signal in this run: the visible workflow surface is now much broader across home automation, visual briefings, accounting intake, job search, support automation, and hardware control.
NextClaw showed fresh repo activity today, while IronClaw, TinyAGI, HiClaw, Poco, PicoClaw, Hermes Agent, and SwarmClaw all remain on very recent release cadences.
The monitored set itself did not need to change: Hermes remains the canonical tracked repo at https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent, and the current taxonomy still fits the market.
whyItMatters: The strongest category signal is still workflow breadth, not raw GitHub churn: OpenClaw's showcase is increasingly evidence that messaging-native assistants are escaping demo-land and touching recurring operational work.
ZeroClaw's same-day stable + beta shipping cadence keeps validating demand for lighter, cheaper, and faster-starting alternatives to the reference implementation.
AstrBot, HiClaw, TinyAGI, and SwarmClaw show that adjacent platform and orchestration layers are not slowing down either, so competition is widening both horizontally and vertically.
IronClaw, NanoClaw, and Poco keep the runtime-trust / isolation axis alive, which matters because security is becoming a first-class product wedge rather than a side concern.
This is still ecosystem deepening, not taxonomy reset: more release velocity, more applied use cases, same top-level strategic map.
